resources.title: Street Sweeping Schedule
resources.keywords: street cleaning sweeping ticket tow zone schedule parking restriction april november
resources.body: Street sweeping runs April 1 through mid-November. Move your car on your zone's posted days to avoid a ticket and tow. Look up your sweeping zone and this year's schedule, or track the sweeper live.

resources.title: Trash, Recycling & Blue Cart Pickup
resources.keywords: garbage trash recycling blue cart pickup collection schedule bulk
resources.body: Look up your block's garbage and blue-cart recycling collection day, and schedule bulk or e-waste pickups.

resources.title: 311 - City Service Requests
resources.keywords: 311 pothole graffiti rodent rat tree light broken complaint service request
resources.body: Report potholes, graffiti, rodents, broken street lights, tree trims, and more. Requests are tracked and you get status updates.

resources.title: CAPS Community Policing Meetings
resources.keywords: caps police beat meeting safety crime community policing district 1st 12th
resources.body: CAPS meetings connect neighbors, police, and the ward team on local safety. The ward spans the 1st (Central) and 12th (Near West) police districts. This week: Beat 113 (Jul 1, 5pm) and Beat 111 (Jul 1, 6pm) at Capital One Cafe, 100 S. State; Beat 112 walking meeting Jul 2 at noon - email the district for location.

resources.title: Private Camera Registration
resources.keywords: security camera register police investigation crime video
resources.body: If your security camera captures the public way, you can voluntarily register it with CPD so detectives can contact you after a nearby crime. Police get no direct access to your system.

resources.title: Know Your Rights & Immigration Resources
resources.keywords: immigration ice rights know your rights icirr family support alerts
resources.body: Request a Know Your Rights training, connect with ICIRR's Family Support Network, and sign up for verified alert networks.

resources.title: Residential Parking Permits & Guest Passes
resources.keywords: parking permit residential zone guest pass daily sticker visitor
resources.body: Residential zone permits and one-day guest passes are issued by the City Clerk - buy online, at the Clerk's office (City Hall, 121 N. LaSalle), or at participating currency exchanges.

resources.title: City Vehicle Sticker
resources.keywords: city sticker vehicle wheel tax renew clerk
resources.body: All Chicago residents with a vehicle need an annual city sticker from the City Clerk. Renew online or in person; late purchase means back-charges and possible tickets.

resources.title: 34th Ward Office - Ald. Bill Conway
resources.keywords: alderman ward office conway contact infrastructure city council 34th
resources.body: Ward office: City Hall, 121 N. LaSalle St., Room 200. Phone 312-744-6820, email bill@ward34.org. The ward team also tables at the West Loop Green City Market on Saturday mornings (9–11am). Closed Fri, Jul 3 for the holiday.

resources.title: City of Chicago Officials
resources.keywords: mayor brandon johnson city clerk anna valencia treasurer contact city hall
resources.body: Mayor Brandon Johnson, City Clerk Anna Valencia, and City Treasurer - all reachable through City Hall at 121 N. LaSalle St. For most city services, start with 311.

resources.title: County, State & Federal Representatives
resources.keywords: congress senator representative state rep cook county preckwinkle davis durbin duckworth district lookup
resources.body: Cook County Board President: Toni Preckwinkle. U.S. House: most of the ward sits in IL-7 (Rep. Danny K. Davis). U.S. Senators: Dick Durbin and Tammy Duckworth. For your exact state legislators (State Rep & State Senator), use the official district lookup - boundaries split the ward.

resources.title: Hospitals Near the Ward
resources.keywords: hospital emergency room er rush stroger ui health northwestern medical
resources.body: The Illinois Medical District sits just west of the ward: Rush University Medical Center (1620 W. Harrison), UI Health (1740 W. Taylor), and Stroger Hospital of Cook County (1969 W. Ogden). Northwestern Memorial serves the east side of downtown.

resources.title: Urgent Care & Clinics
resources.keywords: urgent care clinic walk-in doctor immediate care flu shot
resources.body: Several walk-in urgent care clinics operate in the West Loop and Loop - search current locations and hours near you. For low-cost care, Cook County Health's community clinics serve all patients regardless of insurance.

resources.title: Donate Clothing & Goods
resources.keywords: donate clothing goodwill salvation army donation drop off clothes furniture
resources.body: Hope's Table at the Chicago Temple (First United Methodist, with Grace Episcopal at 637 S. Dearborn) distributes clothing to neighbors in need on Saturday mornings. Goodwill and the Salvation Army both have drop-off locators and pickup scheduling for larger donations.

resources.title: Food Banks & Volunteering
resources.keywords: food bank pantry volunteer greater chicago food depository hunger donate food
resources.body: The Greater Chicago Food Depository welcomes volunteer groups (Indivisible Greater West Loop is going July 10). Hope's Table serves hot Saturday meals downtown and always needs hands.

resources.title: Mary Bartelme Park & Movies in the Park
resources.keywords: mary bartelme park movies casablanca playground dog park sangamon outdoor movie
resources.body: The West Loop's signature park at 115 S. Sangamon - playground (new surface this year), dog-friendly area, and the iconic steel arches. Free sponsored movie screenings run all summer ('Casablanca' screened June 30; watch the feed for the next title). The Park Advisory Council organizes Farmers Market Fun Days and more.

resources.title: Ward Parks: Skinner, Printers Row & Heritage Green
resources.keywords: skinner park printers row park heritage green fieldhouse dog park programs
resources.body: Skinner Park (1331 W. Adams) has a fieldhouse, sports fields, and a dog park. Printers Row Park hosts the South Loop Farmers Market on Saturdays. Find programs, permits, and events for every park.

resources.title: Millennium Park Free Summer Events
resources.keywords: millennium park free concert movie fitness summer music workout
resources.body: DCASE's free summer calendar at Millennium Park includes movies, concerts, and fitness classes just east of the ward - including screenings before Taste of Chicago and celebrations of Chicago house music.

resources.title: Farmers Markets (4 in the ward)
resources.keywords: farmers market green city daley plaza printers row produce saturday thursday tuesday
resources.body: West Loop Green City Market: Saturdays 8am–1pm, 900 W. Monroe (ward team on site 9–11am). South Loop Farmers Market: Saturdays 9am–1pm at Printers Row Park. Daley Plaza Market: Thursdays 7am–2pm. The Farmer at the Green: Tuesdays 11am–3pm, 320 S. Canal.

resources.title: Happy Hour Deals (verified weekly)
resources.keywords: happy hour drinks deals bar specials cocktails beer
resources.body: Venue-verified happy hours across the ward, re-checked on a daily cycle so nothing stale ever shows - currently Caché 310, Monteverde, and Green Street Local.

resources.title: Concerts & Shows in the Ward
resources.keywords: concert music show theater tickets city winery epiphany lyric goodman broadway
resources.body: Live music most nights at City Winery (1200 W. Randolph) and Epiphany Center for the Arts (201 S. Ashland - free Blues Friday July 3). Broadway tours at the Loop's historic houses, opera at the Lyric, and more - every venue links to its live calendar.

resources.title: Thursday Night Beats & Bites
resources.keywords: food trucks live music roosevelt collection thursday summer entertainment
resources.body: Food trucks and live entertainment at Roosevelt Collection's main plaza, Thursdays 5–8pm through July.
